title: Firecraft Safety Products Inventory Management System
summary: Automate inventory tracking, accounting, and ISO report generation for FireCraft Safety Products — a manufacturer of structural fire gloves and exclusive distributor of an advanced gas detector. Products are manufactured overseas, fulfilled through a dealer network, and currently tracked entirely by hand using a SAGE accounting system. Work with CS alumnus Rob Twitchell to define warehouse efficiency requirements and implement the full inventory management system.

title: OCLC Project
summary: Develop modern library software services for OCLC (Online Computer Library Center) — a global nonprofit managing WorldCat, the world's largest bibliographic catalog with roughly 14 petabytes of data. The project will involve modern software development tools and data analytics. A more detailed description is forthcoming from industry contact T.J. Godsey.

title: GROB Systems: Intelligent Robot Bin Picking Automation
summary: Build a fully functional vision-guided bin picking system where a robot and 3D camera identify and retrieve randomly oriented parts from a bulk container without collisions. Workstreams cover mechanical design in Siemens NX, electrical schematics and pneumatic actuation, PLC programming and controls integration, FANUC ROBOGUIDE robot programming, Siemens Process/Plant simulation, and MechMind 3D vision integration. The team delivers a working bin-picking cell with a complete digital twin.

title: Argonne National Lab Quantum Instrumentation Project
summary: Design and implement a robust control plane and GUI for the Argonne Quantum Network (ArQNet) testbed — a telecom-grade quantum local area network. Harden a proof-of-concept orchestrator built on the MatSci lab automation framework for reliability and external user access, enabling resource reservation of control computers, photon sources, and detectors. Approximately five students with software engineering experience needed; a mix of CpE and CS is ideal as the software interfaces directly with quantum hardware.

title: eSight Go Computer Mouse Design for the Visually Impaired
summary: Design and build an all-in-one computer mouse that simultaneously controls a standard PC and the Bluetooth vision controls (zoom, color filters, contrast) of eSight Go augmented-vision glasses worn by a user with X-Linked Retinitis Pigmentosa. The solution should let the user intuitively coordinate mouse movements and vision adjustments together. Patent co-authorship opportunity with ONU.

title: Cenovus: Wireless Instrumentation and OT Infrastructure
summary: Develop a secure operational technology (OT) network for Cenovus that communicates plant control and process data wirelessly across the facility. Determine wireless network coverage in the field, design a secure-by-design wireless network architecture, and select appropriate wireless transmitters and network hardware. Process data is sensitive, so OT security must be built into every design decision.

title: Blank Part Cutoff Sorting
summary: Implement and train a Cognex AI vision system to reliably identify surface defects (scratches, blemishes) on metal stampings as they exit a stamping press at Ohio Valley Manufacturing. Then design and prototype an automated system that removes unacceptable parts from the production line. Must operate at production speeds, require zero human intervention, and be portable by forklift so it can be reconfigured for different products.

title: Padrone's Pizza Automated Adjustable Pizza Dough Rounder
summary: Design and build an affordable automated pizza dough rolling machine for a small local restaurant that processes 300 lbs of dough per week. Must produce multiple pizza sizes (7, 10, 12, 14, and 16 inches) using adjustable motor-controlled rollers, operate on 120VAC/15A, fit within 24x24 inches, use food-safe materials, and require minimal operator training.

title: GPI Project
summary: GPI's Kenton, OH facility produces over 10 million paper cups per day. Past capstone projects have addressed automated case sealers, pneumatic cup conveyance systems, and devices to safely move 500-lb rolls of paper. The exact 2026-27 project is TBD but will be in the controls, mechanical design, or mechatronics space. Dr. Funke is on sabbatical at GPI and will help finalize the scope.

title: Norada Lanes Bowling Control and Monitoring System
summary: Redesign the aging control and monitoring systems for the lane machines at Norada Lanes, a small 8-lane bowling alley experiencing frequent pin-setting failures, sweep malfunctions, and ball jams. Replace or augment existing controls with PLCs or microcontrollers and add sensor/camera-based monitoring to detect abnormal conditions early. Log performance data and report issues to a mechanic dashboard for preventive maintenance.

title: Healthwise Mobile Health Clinic Data Services
summary: Build a new data storage and analysis platform for ONU's Mobile Health Clinic, which serves underserved populations across Hardin, Allen, and Auglaize counties. Replace fragmented Excel spreadsheets with a system providing HIPAA-compliant data entry with validation, schema management, a stakeholder-facing data visualization dashboard, and integration with an existing route optimization pipeline.

title: Pharmacy Patient Simulator
summary: Develop a software system that generates realistic simulated datasets of 100+ unique patients with varying disease states and lab values (A1c, proteinuria, medication adherence) based on configurable probability inputs. Output to Excel or similar format to support Dr. Jewell's pharmacy student training course. A sample document of anonymous patient data will be provided for reference.

title: Low-Power Mesh-Based Sensor Network for Remote Monitoring
summary: Design and evaluate a small-scale distributed sensing system with multiple mesh-connected low-power nodes and a central gateway for outdoor or remote environments without reliable power or Wi-Fi. Nodes support flexible sensor interfacing via UART, SPI, I2C, and analog inputs with persistent data storage. The gateway aggregates all node data and connects to a host computer for configuration and retrieval.

title: Fashion Interface — Virtual Fitting System
summary: Develop a prototype virtual fitting system using a 3D scanner that captures a user's body and converts it to a digital 3D model. Integrate simple clothing representations to visualize how different sizes and styles fit, and deliver the results through an interactive interface. End-to-end pipeline spans data acquisition, 3D model processing, clothing integration, and user interaction.

title: Hardware Password Manager
summary: Design and build a USB-style hardware device that securely stores cryptographic keys and unlocks them via biometric authentication (fingerprint sensor) using a passkey-style approach based on public-key cryptography. Develop both hardware and software components including secure key storage, biometric interfacing, and host system communication. Explore hardware-software co-design tradeoffs in cost, performance, and security.

title: Smart Trash Sorting Attachment for Standard Waste Bins
summary: Design and develop a smart sorting mechanism that attaches to a standard trash bin, allowing users to drop any waste into a single input opening while the system automatically sorts materials (recyclables vs. landfill) internally. Improves recycling rates without requiring behavioral change from users.

title: FridgeApp — Smart Refrigerator Inventory System
summary: Build an app-connected smart refrigerator system that automatically scans and records groceries as they are placed inside, providing a real-time digital inventory accessible from anywhere via mobile app. Also monitors internal conditions (temperature changes, door-open events) with push notifications.

title: CleanSlate: Autonomous Whiteboard Cleaning Robot
summary: Design and build a compact autonomous robotic system that navigates the surface of a classroom whiteboard and systematically erases written content without human intervention. Addresses inefficiencies from tight professor schedules between back-to-back classes and the learning disruption of partially erased boards.

title: IEEE Micromouse
summary: Design a tiny autonomous electromechanical robot from scratch (not a kit) that uses onboard sensors and mapping algorithms to navigate a maze as fast as possible. The team must join IEEE and compete at minimum in the IEEE Region 2 competition. Covers mechanical design, sensor integration, embedded programming, and autonomous pathfinding algorithms. NOTE: IEEE membership required.

title: Robotic Football Advanced Center
summary: Develop a next-generation robotic Center robot compatible with ONU's new autonomous Quarterback for the CRFC competition. The old Center has physical interface limitations with the new QB model. The project is open-ended by design — no single optimal configuration exists for snap mechanics, drivetrain layout, or control architecture — opening the door for innovation across mechanical design, sensing, actuation, and closed-loop control.

title: SafeSmart Bike Rack
summary: Create a smart bike rack with a built-in locking mechanism: the rider aligns their bike frame with a slot in one pole, scans their student ID, and a strong metal rod automatically extends through the bike frame into the opposite pole — making removal impossible without damaging the bike. Retraction requires the same student ID scan, preventing unauthorized release.

title: IoT-Based Smart Waste Management System for the JLK Building
summary: Design an IoT platform for the JLK Building that detects when individual trash cans reach capacity and automatically notifies janitorial staff for timely replacement. Work with building stakeholders to define system requirements and develop a real-time monitoring dashboard with waste-level visibility across all cans in the building.
contact: Dr. Ahmed Ammar / Dr. Ben McPheron

title: Smart Vent — Retrofit HVAC Airflow Control Device
summary: Design a retrofit device that installs on existing HVAC ventilation registers to independently control airflow in individual rooms using local temperature sensing or manual user adjustment — no integration with the building's main HVAC controller required. Solves hot and cold zones in homes and dormitories at a fraction of the cost of full smart-thermostat zoning systems.
